name: Cantabrian Route: San Cibrao and Cervo Loop
name_original: San Cibrao – vuelta de la costa gallega
description: This moderate 18.1-mile mountain biking route along the Galician coast offers stunning ocean views and passes through San Cibrao fishing vil
region_intro: Mountain bike trails around Costa, a region in Lugo, Spain, offer diverse terrain for riders. The landscape features a mix of coastal paths, beaches, and river valleys, alongside inland hills and peaks. Mountain biking routes often traverse through forested areas and offer views of the Cantabrian Sea. This variety provides options for different skill levels, from moderate coastal loops to challenging climbs in the interior. Best mountain bike trails around Costa The most popular mountain bike trail is Ribadeo – Circular por Monte Mondigo y la Playa dos Castros, a 27.3 miles (44.0 km) difficult route that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This trail explores Monte Mondigo and the coastal area of Playa dos Castros. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Foz – vuelta por Monte de Lebre sendero del río Ouro, a moderate 15.5 miles (25.0 km) path. This route winds through Monte de Lebre and follows the Ouro River. Local mountain bikers also love the Cantabrian Route: San Cibrao and Cervo Loop, a 18.1 miles (29.2 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 2 hours 44 minutes. Mountain biking around Costa is defined by coastal paths, river valleys, and inland hills. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate loops to more challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Costa are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Costa's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available in Costa, Lugo?
A: There are nearly 100 mountain bike trails around Costa, Lugo, offering a wide range of options for riders. The komoot community has explored these routes over 600 times, providing valuable insights and ratings.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo?
A: Mountain biking in Costa, Lugo, features diverse terrain. You'll find a mix of coastal paths, scenic beaches, and lush river valleys, alongside challenging inland hills and peaks. Many routes traverse forested areas and offer stunning views of the Cantabrian Sea.

Q: What are the difficulty levels of mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo?
A: The trails in Costa, Lugo, cater to various skill levels. You can find easy routes, but the majority (around 69) are moderate. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are also 24 difficult trails available, including routes with significant elevation changes.

Q: Are there any long-distance mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo?
A: Yes, Costa, Lugo, offers several longer mountain bike trails. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993674597/mondonedo-and-o-cadramon-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo and O Cadramón Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 32.3-mile (52.0 km) route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10606804" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Roque Viewpoint loop from Viveiro-Apeadoiro&lt;/a&gt; covers 33.1 miles (53.2 km) with substantial climbing.

Q: What do other mountain bikers enjoy most about mountain biking in Costa, Lugo?
A: The mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo, are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the varied terrain, from coastal views to inland climbs, and the well-maintained paths that offer a rewarding experience.

Q: Are there mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo, that offer coastal views?
A: Absolutely. Many mountain bike routes in Costa, Lugo, incorporate coastal paths and offer breathtaking views of the Cantabrian Sea. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994345728/cantabrian-route-san-cibrao-and-cervo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cantabrian Route: San Cibrao and Cervo Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a great example, leading through stunning coastal landscapes.

Q: Can I find circular mountain bike routes in Costa, Lugo?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo, are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993666924/ribadeo-circular-por-monte-mondigo-y-la-playa-dos-castros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadeo – Circular por Monte Mondigo y la Playa dos Castros&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994341338/foz-vuelta-por-monte-de-lebre-sendero-del-r%C3%ADo-ouro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz – vuelta por Monte de Lebre sendero del río Ouro&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural attractions can I see along mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo?
A: While mountain biking in Costa, Lugo, you can encounter several natural attractions. Notable natural monuments include the famous &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/526099" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Beach of the Cathedrals&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2833444" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Praia das Illas&lt;/a&gt;, both known for their unique rock formations. You might also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3163847" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa de los Castros Natural Rock Arch&lt;/a&gt; and the scenic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/284283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuciño do Porco Point&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks near the mountain bike trails in Costa, Lugo?
A: Yes, the region offers several interesting landmarks. You could visit the town of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359284" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ribadeo&lt;/a&gt;, explore the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/359296" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of San Salvador&lt;/a&gt;, or see the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/368178" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo Cathedral&lt;/a&gt;. These sites provide a glimpse into the rich history and culture of the area.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Costa, Lugo?
A: The region experiences a mild climate, but for the most pleasant mountain biking conditions, late spring through early autumn (May to October) generally offers warmer temperatures and less rainfall. However, coastal routes can be enjoyed year-round, weather permitting.

Q: Are there any mountain bike trails that follow rivers in Costa, Lugo?
A: Yes, some trails in Costa, Lugo, wind alongside rivers, offering a different scenic experience. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e994341338/foz-vuelta-por-monte-de-lebre-sendero-del-r%C3%ADo-ouro" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz – vuelta por Monte de Lebre sendero del río Ouro&lt;/a&gt; is an example of a route that follows the Ouro River.

Q: Can I find challenging mountain bike routes with significant elevation in Costa, Lugo?
A: For riders looking for a challenge, Costa, Lugo, offers difficult routes with substantial elevation gain. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e993674597/mondonedo-and-o-cadramon-peak-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondoñedo and O Cadramón Peak Loop&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, features over 1500 meters of ascent, providing a demanding ride through inland hills.
tips: Located at the mouth of the Covo River, this small fishing village on the Galician north coast has several important sites such as the Museo Provincial do Mar and the Punta Atalaia lighthouse, as well as Torno beach.
An important settlement from the Iron Age and prehistory on the Galician north coast. The tip of Cape Burela is also accessible here from the seafront. Spectacular seascape and views.
Great trails along the Xunco and Sargadelos river including the Sargadelos botanical and an old factory that uses the energy of the rivers water, a historical museum and a fortress

faqs.question: How difficult is the San Cibrao – vuelta de la costa gallega mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: This route is rated as moderate, demanding good physical fitness and advanced riding skills. Its technical difficulty is approximately STS S2, meaning you can expect small obstacles like protruding roots and stones, with some uneven terrain. It combines roads, paths, and singletrack.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this mountain bike trail?
faqs.answer: The trail features a varied terrain, including roads, paths, and singletrack sections. While specific details for rocky or rooty sections are not provided, general mountain biking in Galicia can include steep sections, rocks, and roots. The STS S2 rating suggests small obstacles like roots and stones.

faqs.question: What points of interest will I see along the San Cibrao – vuelta de la costa gallega route?
faqs.answer: Along the route, you'll encounter several notable features. These include the charming San Cibrao Fishing Village, the historical Cape and castro of Burela, and the Old Sargadelos Pottery Factory. The trail also offers stunning coastal scenery with beaches, cliffs, and estuaries.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this route?
faqs.answer: As a coastal trail in Galicia, the best time to ride is generally during the milder, drier months to fully enjoy the scenery and avoid heavy rains. Spring and early autumn often offer pleasant temperatures and less crowded conditions.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the San Cibrao – vuelta de la costa gallega trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking areas for this exact trail are not detailed, San Cibrao is a fishing village, and you can typically find public parking within or near the village to begin your ride. Look for designated parking zones or street parking that allows for longer stays.

faqs.question: Is the San Cibrao – vuelta de la costa gallega trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: There is no specific information indicating restrictions against dogs on this mountain bike route. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in populated areas, near wildlife, or on shared paths, and to clean up after them.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this mountain bike route?
faqs.answer: No specific permits or entrance fees are mentioned or typically required for mountain biking this route along the Galician coast. It is generally accessible to the public.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails. Notably, it covers 44% of the Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico and Mtb Camiño natural do Roteiro do Cantábrico. You'll also find smaller sections of the Paseo Marítimo de Burela, Camiño de Nardión, and Pista de Guerreiro, among others.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or stay near the trail?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the trail passes through and near the San Cibrao Fishing Village and Burela, you will find various options for cafes, restaurants, and accommodation. These coastal towns offer amenities for visitors.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty rating and STS S2 technical classification, this trail is generally not recommended for beginners or young families. It requires good physical fitness and advanced riding skills due to potential obstacles and varied terrain.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this trail?
faqs.answer: For this moderate mountain bike route, you should bring appropriate safety gear, including a helmet, gloves, and knee pads. Carry enough water, snacks, a basic repair kit, and a fully charged phone. Sun protection is also recommended for coastal riding.
